chapter_title: About Forbiddance Mourning more than Three

hadith_entries.text:
1023. Narrated Homayd ibn Nafe'e that Zaynab bint Umm
Salama narrated to him the following three Hadiths. She said;
'I entered on Umm Habeeba, the wife of the Prophet, when
her father Abu-Sofyan ibn Harb has died. She asked for some
perfume that had yellowness and applied it to a slave girl then
she applied it to herself. Then, she said; 'By Allah, I am not
in need of perfume, but I had heard the Messenger of Allah
saying: "It is not lawful for a woman that believes in Allah
and the Last Day to mourn³2 over a deceased more than three
32 Mourning over the deceased does mean lament as some
people practice. It is only the abandonment of embellishment.
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
days except for the husband. She mourns for four months and
ten days.' (At-Termizi).
1024. Zaynab also said; 'I entered on Zaynab bint Jahsh
when her brother died. She asked for perfume and applied it
to her, then she said; 'By Allah, I am not in need of perfume,
but I had heard the Messenger of Allah saying: "It is not
lawful for a woman that believes in Allah and the Last Day to
33 over a deceased more than three nights except for the
husband. She mourns for four months and ten days."
(At-Termizi).
mourn
اللَّهِ
33 Mourning over the deceased does mean lament as some
people practice. It is only the abandonment of embellishment.
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
1025. Zaynab also said; 'I heard my mother Umm Salama
saying that a woman came to the Messenger of Allah and
said; 'O Messenger of Allah! My daughter's husband has died
and her eyes got soared, would she apply the kohl on them?"
The Messenger of Allah said: "No." He said this twice or
thrice. Then he said: "It is four months and ten days and one
of you used to stay for a complete year³4.” (At-Termizi).
Abu-Issa said; 'The Hadith of Zaynab is Hasan Saheeh
and the people of knowledge of the Prophet's companions
acted on it. They said that she whose usband has died should
shun applying perfume and other cosmetics during her Idda.
This is the opinion of Sofyanoth-Thawri, Malek ibn Anas,
Al-Shafe'e, Ahmed, and Ishaq.'
